verification_steps_after_revision: Confirm CSVs parse cleanly and values match workbook cells.
Confirm formulas_dump lists all formulas used in decision logic and references only packet-derived input cells.

planned_changes: Insert an 'Audit' sheet into solvent_degradation_workbook.xlsx that enumerates key decision cells and their formulas in plain text (format: sheet,cell,formula,short_description).
Export core computed sheets (Measurements, Action_Analytics, Decision_Table, Validation) as CSV files and include them alongside the .xlsx in artifacts to allow automated parsing without openpyxl.
Create a 'formulas_dump.txt' containing every formula from the workbook in a parseable format (sheet,cell,formula).
Update reclaiming_gate_memo.md with a verification checklist mapping each decision (D1/D2/D3 and package-level pass/fail) to the exact sheet names and cell ranges used to compute them.

Produce and attach a parse proof: either an openpyxl parsing log (openpyxl_parse_log.txt) showing successful formula reads, or a screenshot of the workbook opened in a common spreadsheet application showing the Audit sheet and a few representative formula cells.

Re-run the verification step and attach the CSVs/formula dump so the automated verifier can perform cell-level checks; include in the revised attempt a brief note in final_message.txt that points verifiers to the new audit artifacts.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve verifier-flagged inability to perform automated formula-level verification due to missing openpyxl and provide parseable artifacts and explicit cell-to-decision mappings so future automated verifiers can validate formulas and numeric provenance.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables present and well-documented: the memo clearly enumerates packet provenance, thresholds, action effects, per-row classifications, sensitivity checks, and a conservative recommendation (NEEDS_REVIEW). The grader and deterministic prechecks passed. However, automated cell-level formula verification was not possible in this environment because openpyxl was unavailable; the trace shows a manual XLSX XML write claiming formula-backed cells, but that claim is not equivalent to an independently parsed verification. The verifier flagged this gap and recommended a parseable audit artifact or human inspection to confirm formulas and exact cell references.
